original_hypothesis.title: Bonding.launch() Permanently Broken Due to Double NonReentrant Guard
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Bonding.launch() calls launchFor() internally, but both functions carry the nonReentrant modifier from ReentrancyGuardUpgradeable. When launch() enters the guard (status=ENTERED), the internal call to launchFor() attempts to re-enter the same guard and reverts. This permanently breaks launch(), forcing all token launches through launchFor() which accepts an arbitrary creator address parameter. Anyone can attribute token creation to any address, affecting who receives graduation rewards and AgentTax creator fee distributions (99% of tax revenue by default).
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a clear code defect. The launch() function can never succeed. The economic impact is that launchFor() allows any caller to set any creator address, enabling creator attribution manipulation that redirects significant fee revenue.

proof_of_signal.pattern: launch() has nonReentrant and calls launchFor() which also has nonReentrant — nested guard always reverts

preconditions: Anyone can call launchFor() with arbitrary creator parameter
Token graduates and creator receives tax revenue

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Both launch() and launchFor() have nonReentrant from same ReentrancyGuardUpgradeable inheritance
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker sets creator=attacker_address in launchFor() for a token they expect to be popular, capturing 99% of future tax distributions
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: 99% of all agent tax revenue for that token goes to attacker-controlled creator address

economic_questions: How much tax revenue does a typical graduated agent generate?
Can creator be changed after launch via AgentTax.updateCreator?
Are there active tokens launched via launchFor with incorrect creator attribution?
validation_requirements: Confirm launch() reverts with double nonReentrant
Test launchFor() with arbitrary creator address
Trace creator address through graduation to tax distribution

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: purchaseAmount (fee + initial buy) in asset tokens
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: 99% of all future agent tax distributions for the launched token
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Depends on token popularity — potentially thousands of % over time

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When launching tokens expected to become popular
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Minimal market impact; social engineering impact on perceived creator identity

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Legitimate token creators who lose creator attribution
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Cumulative tax revenue for affected agents

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify launch() always reverts; verify creator propagation to AgentTax

validation_result.mechanism: nested nonReentrant guard causing permanent revert
validation_result.impact_summary: Bonding.launch() is permanently broken because it internally calls launchFor() and both carry the nonReentrant modifier from the same ReentrancyGuardUpgradeable. Users must use launchFor() which accepts an arbitrary creator address, enabling anyone to attribute token creation to any address and capture 99% of future tax distributions.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The Bonding contract inherits ReentrancyGuardUpgradeable and uses the nonReentrant modifier on both launch() and launchFor(). When launch() is called, the nonReentrant modifier sets the reentrancy guard status to ENTERED. The function body then calls launchFor() directly (an internal call in Solidity). The nonReentrant modifier on launchFor() checks the guard status, finds it is ENTERED, and reverts. This makes launch() permanently unusable.

The impact is significant because launchFor() is public and accepts an arbitrary 'creator' parameter. The creator address is passed through to agentFactory.initFromBondingCurve() during graduation, setting the founder of the agent. In AgentTax._swapForAsset(), the creator/founder receives creatorFee = assetReceived - (assetReceived * feeRate / DENOM), which with feeRate=100 and DENOM=10000 is 99% of all swapped tax revenue.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The code evidence is unambiguous. Both launch() at line 110 and launchFor() at line 122 of Bonding.sol carry the nonReentrant modifier. launch() directly calls launchFor() at line 120. In Solidity, internal calls to public functions still execute their modifiers. The ReentrancyGuardUpgradeable nonReentrant modifier checks a storage slot for ENTERED status and reverts if found, which is guaranteed when called from within another nonReentrant-guarded function.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: launch() has nonReentrant modifier and calls launchFor() which also has nonReentrant. The reentrancy guard is already set to ENTERED when launchFor() checks it, causing an unconditional revert. This function can never succeed.

launchFor() is public with nonReentrant and accepts an arbitrary creator address. Since launch() is broken, this is the only way to launch tokens. Any caller can set any address as creator, which determines who receives 99% of AgentTax distributions after graduation.

With feeRate=100 and DENOM=10000, creator receives assetReceived - 1% = 99% of all swapped tax revenue. The creator address originates from the founder set during initFromBondingCurve, which comes from the creator parameter in launchFor().

calling_sequence: 1. User calls Bonding.launch() with token parameters
2. nonReentrant modifier on launch() sets _status = ENTERED (ReentrancyGuardUpgradeable storage)
3. launch() body executes: return launchFor(..., msg.sender)
4. launchFor() nonReentrant modifier checks _status, finds ENTERED, reverts with ReentrancyGuardReentrantCall()
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: Bonding.sol:110 launch() [nonReentrant sets ENTERED] -> Bonding.sol:120 calls launchFor() -> Bonding.sol:131 launchFor() [nonReentrant checks status, finds ENTERED] -> REVERT. The alternative path via direct launchFor() call: any caller can set arbitrary creator address (Bonding.sol:131 creator parameter) -> creator stored in tokenInfo (Bonding.sol:171) -> passed to agentFactory.initFromBondingCurve during graduation (Bonding.sol:247 _token.creator) -> stored as founder in AgentNft -> AgentTax._getTaxRecipient reads founder (AgentTax.sol:142) -> 99% of tax revenue sent to creator (AgentTax.sol:170-174)

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided for Bonding.sol specifically. The defect is inherent to the code regardless of deployment configuration.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Attack scenario is straightforward: 1) Attacker identifies a token concept likely to become popular. 2) Attacker calls launchFor() with themselves as creator, paying the launch fee. 3) Token trades on the bonding curve and eventually graduates. 4) AgentTax distributions for the graduated agent send 99% of tax revenue to the attacker (the false creator). The legitimate intended flow where launch() uses msg.sender as creator is broken, so there's no legitimate way to enforce caller=creator attribution.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity. The launch() function is permanently non-functional, which is a clear code defect. The only alternative, launchFor(), allows arbitrary creator attribution. The economic impact is that anyone can claim creatorship of tokens and receive 99% of all future AgentTax distributions for that agent. This affects the entire bonding curve launch mechanism and all downstream tax revenue distribution.
recommended_mitigations: Remove the nonReentrant modifier from launch() since it only delegates to launchFor() which already has the guard
Alternatively, extract the shared logic into an internal function _launch() without nonReentrant, and have both launch() and launchFor() call it with their own nonReentrant modifiers
Consider adding access control or validation to launchFor() to restrict who can set the creator parameter, or require msg.sender == creator
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy Bonding contract with full initialization. 2. Call launch() with valid parameters and sufficient asset token balance. 3. Observe revert with ReentrancyGuardReentrantCall error. 4. Call launchFor() directly with attacker address as creator - observe success. 5. Drive token to graduation threshold via buy(). 6. Verify AgentTax distributes 99% of tax to the attacker-specified creator address.
uncertainties: The exact version of OpenZeppelin ReentrancyGuardUpgradeable used - however all versions since the introduction of the ENTERED/NOT_ENTERED pattern will exhibit this behavior
Whether the protocol team has a separate UI/backend that always calls launchFor() directly, bypassing launch() - but this doesn't fix the code defect or the arbitrary creator issue
additional_testing_needed: Verify the Solidity compiler version (0.8.20+) behavior for internal calls to public functions with modifiers
Test that launchFor() with arbitrary creator propagates correctly through graduation to AgentTax distributions
Verify AgentTax.updateCreator() cannot be used by legitimate parties to reclaim creator status after malicious attribution
